title: Make release identity and self-onboard projection parity fail closed
action: Implement D1 and D3 in the onboarding engine and fixture suite. The authoritative source markers are skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, and .codex-plugin/plugin.json; every required value must be numeric, readable, and equal before target mutation. Valid greenfield targets may lack .bee/onboarding.json, .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs, and project skill projections; after apply every applicable target surface for the selected distribution mode must equal the validated source tuple. Repair canonical source self-onboarding so a release-isolated source can refresh project projections rather than silently self-skipping, but prove it in isolated fixtures and do not refresh the live .agents/.claude roots in this mixed dirty tree. Keep source/target overlap and downgrade protections fail-closed.

truths: The canonical state version and both package manifest versions are readable numeric and equal before any target change
Missing unreadable or unequal authoritative source versions refuse before changing a target

A greenfield target may begin without onboarding runtime or project projections and succeeds only after .bee/onboarding.json, .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs, and distribution-applicable project projections equal the validated tuple
Fixture self-onboarding upgrades stale project projections and a second run reports up_to_date
The mixed live source tree is not broadly copied into project projection roots before release isolation

artifacts.substantive: strict release identity and source-self projection refresh without bypassing overlap safety
behavioral regression cases for mixed tuple, valid greenfield absence, stale projections, zero mutation, and idempotency

key_links: The tuple from skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs plus both package manifests is the sole version written into onboarding and compared with target runtime/projections
The self-source case refreshes fixture project projections only after release/source identity and overlap checks pass
prohibitions: Do not weaken downgrade symlink alias or source-overlap refusal
Do not delete foreign or non-bee project skills
Do not refresh the live .agents/skills or .claude/skills trees from the mixed dirty canonical tree
Do not touch unfinished wait-loop or worktree-isolation files
